meta.json_run_notes: Verified, corrected, and merged 7 MA statutes from previous JSON batches. Corrected fee-shifting types, eliminated parent taxonomy slugs, handled missing limit_ambiguous fields and omitted missing/empty fields properly.

records.official_name: Retaliation against employees reporting violations of law or risks to public health, safety or environment; remedies

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Covers discharge, suspension, demotion and other adverse employment action affecting the terms and conditions of employment. Written notice to a supervisor is generally required before disclosure to a public body under subsection (b)(1), subject to the statutory exceptions.

enforcement.adverse_action_details: Also covers other adverse employment action in the terms and conditions of employment.
enforcement.remedies_details: The statute provides common-law tort remedies in addition to legal or equitable relief, including three times lost wages, benefits and other remuneration, plus interest.

burden_of_proof.burden_of_proof_details: Employee must show disclosure or activity based on reasonable belief that the activity, policy, or practice violates law/rule/regulation or poses risk to public health, safety, or environment.

attached_citations: Bennett v. City of Holyoke || applies written-notice requirement || https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/appellate-courts/F3/362/1/632823/ || law.justia.com || high
Dirrane v. Brookline Police Dep't || limits bypass of notice rule || https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/appellate-courts/F3/315/65/633064/ || law.justia.com || high
Saunders v. Town of Hull || clarifies subsection (b)(1) framework || https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/appellate-courts/ca1/17-1174/17-1174-2017-10-27.html || law.justia.com || high

records._review_notes: This is the central Massachusetts whistleblower statute. The written notice requirement is treated as a procedural bar rather than administrative exhaustion.

records.official_name: Health care providers; protection from retaliatory action by health care facilities

legal_basis.protected_class_details: Protects licensed health care providers under chapter 112 and other health care providers performing care-related services under the control of a health care facility.
legal_basis.disclosure_targets_details: The statute defines 'public body' broadly to include public agencies, legislatures and law-enforcement bodies.
legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Covers refusal to hire, termination of a contractual agreement, discharge, suspension, demotion, harassment, denial of promotion, layoff and other adverse action affecting the terms and conditions of employment.

enforcement.adverse_action_details: Also expressly covers refusal to hire, denial of promotion, layoff and other adverse action affecting terms and conditions of employment.
enforcement.remedies_details: Prevailing plaintiffs may access all common-law tort remedies. The attorney general may bring a public-interest action, and the court may impose a civil penalty of up to $10,000 per violation.

attached_citations: Blanchard v. Steward Carney Hospital, Inc. || clarifies mandatory-reporting overlap || https://law.justia.com/cases/massachusetts/supreme-court/2017/sjc-12141.html || law.justia.com || high

Commodore v. Genesis Health Ventures, Inc. || interprets health-care facility coverage || https://law.justia.com/cases/massachusetts/court-of-appeals/volumes/63/63massappct57.html || law.justia.com || moderate

Romero v. UHS of Westwood Pembroke, Inc. || applies provider-protection requirements || https://law.justia.com/cases/massachusetts/court-of-appeals/volumes/72/72massappct539.html || law.justia.com || moderate

records._review_notes: Specific to health care facilities and providers. Includes participation in peer review or filing incident reports on unsafe care.

records.official_name: Employers preventing employees from acting to further false claim actions; liability
records.common_name: Massachusetts False Claims Act anti-retaliation provision

legal_basis.protected_class_details: Expressly protects an employee, contractor or agent, and also covers acts done by a person associated with the employee, contractor or agent.
legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Protects disclosures to government or law-enforcement agencies and lawful acts in furtherance of a false-claims action or other efforts to stop violations.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: No filing deadline was confirmed in the text of section 5J itself; derived from general tort limitations.

enforcement.adverse_action_details: Also covers threats and other discrimination in the terms and conditions of employment.
enforcement.remedies_details: Also provides interest on back pay and compensation for special damages sustained as a result of the discrimination.

records._review_notes: This is the retaliation section of the Massachusetts False Claims Act, not the entire false-claims scheme.

records.official_name: Employees seeking rights under provisions of this chapter; discharge or discrimination; punishment

legal_basis.protected_class_details: Applies to employees seeking rights under the wages and hours provisions of chapter 149.
legal_basis.disclosure_targets_details: Expressly covers complaints to the attorney general or any other person, assistance in an investigation under the chapter, instituting a proceeding or testifying in a proceeding.
legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Covers being penalized in any way for seeking rights under the wage-and-hour provisions of chapter 149, including discharge and other discrimination.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: The three-year period and private action mechanism are derived from chapter 149, section 150 rather than stated in section 148A itself.
statute_of_limitations.exhaustion_details: An employee claiming to be aggrieved proceeds under chapter 149, section 150, which generally requires filing a complaint with the attorney general and waiting 90 days unless the attorney general assents sooner.

enforcement.adverse_action_details: The section also covers being penalized or otherwise discriminated against for seeking wage-and-hour rights.
enforcement.remedies_details: Civil citations and orders are available through section 27C, while the private action and damages route is derived from section 150.

attached_citations: Smith v. Winter Place LLC || confirms wage-complaint retaliation scope || https://law.justia.com/cases/massachusetts/supreme-court/volumes/447/447mass363.html || law.justia.com || high
Fraelick v. PerkettPR, Inc. || clarifies reasonable-belief protection || https://law.justia.com/cases/massachusetts/court-of-appeals/volumes/83/83massappct698.html || law.justia.com || moderate
Parker v. EnerNOC, Inc. || confirms trebling of lost wages || https://law.justia.com/cases/massachusetts/supreme-court/volumes/484/484mass128.html || law.justia.com || high

records._review_notes: This is a retaliation protection embedded in Massachusetts wage-and-hour enforcement. Limits and exhaustion come from section 150.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Broadly prohibits discharge or discrimination against employees complaining of wage violations.

records._review_notes: Focuses on wage protections. Mandatory liquidated damages often apply to wage-related claims in MA.

records.official_name: Rights to Return to Employment; Qualified Handicapped Persons; Discrimination Prohibited

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Prohibits discrimination against an employee for exercising rights under the workers' compensation act.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: Derived from general tort statute of limitations as the specific section does not name an independent deadline.

records._review_notes: Protects injured workers. Note that the statute of limitations is derived from ch. 260 § 2A.

records.official_name: Reports of Abuse, Neglect or Misappropriation of Property of Patients or Residents

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Protection from retaliatory discharge or discrimination for filing a report of abuse in long-term care facilities.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: Derived from general personal injury/tort limitations.

records._review_notes: Mandated reporting for elderly care facility staff. Actual damages mapped to compensatory-damages.

error_details: MA-12-5J: SOL is derived from general limitations, set limit_ambiguous to true.
MA-149-148A: SOL is derived from section 150, set limit_ambiguous to true.
MA-152-75B: SOL is derived from general tort limitations, set limit_ambiguous to true.
MA-111-72G: SOL is derived from general personal injury/tort limitations, set limit_ambiguous to true.
MA-12-5J: Citations omitted due to lack of verifiable URL from approved sources.
MA-151-19: Citations omitted due to lack of verifiable URL from approved sources.
MA-152-75B: Citations omitted due to lack of verifiable URL from approved sources.
MA-111-72G: Citations omitted due to lack of verifiable URL from approved sources.
